1.label: LOW
1.description: Mild or cosmetic condition. Usually self-limiting.
1.advice: Monitor the condition. Consult a dermatologist if it worsens.

2.label: MODERATE
2.advice: Schedule an appointment with a dermatologist for evaluation and treatment.

3.label: HIGH
3.description: Significant medical condition. May cause complications if untreated.
3.advice: Visit a doctor promptly. Do not delay treatment.

4.label: CRITICAL
4.description: Potentially malignant or life-threatening. Urgent care required.
4.advice: Seek IMMEDIATE medical attention. This condition may be serious.

Acne and Rosacea Photos.treatment: Topical retinoids, benzoyl peroxide, azelaic acid, antibiotics for rosacea
Acne and Rosacea Photos.notes: Common inflammatory skin condition. Rarely serious but can be chronic.

Seborrheic Keratoses and other Benign Tu.treatment: Usually no treatment needed; cryotherapy or curettage for cosmetic removal
Seborrheic Keratoses and other Benign Tu.notes: Benign wart-like growths. No malignant potential.

Urticaria Hives.treatment: Antihistamines (cetirizine, loratadine), corticosteroids for severe cases
Urticaria Hives.notes: Allergic reaction. Can become chronic (>6 weeks = chronic urticaria).

Eczema Photos.treatment: Emollients, topical corticosteroids, tacrolimus, dupilumab for severe cases
Eczema Photos.notes: Chronic inflammatory condition. Requires ongoing management.

Atopic Dermatitis Photos.treatment: Moisturizers, topical steroids, calcineurin inhibitors, biologics (dupilumab)
Atopic Dermatitis Photos.notes: Often co-occurs with asthma and allergic rhinitis (atopic triad).

Psoriasis pictures Lichen Planus and rel.notes: Chronic autoimmune condition. Psoriasis linked to psoriatic arthritis.

Nail Fungus and other Nail Disease.treatment: Oral terbinafine/itraconazole, topical ciclopirox for mild cases
Nail Fungus and other Nail Disease.notes: Fungal nail infection is contagious via shared surfaces. Slow to resolve.

Poison Ivy Photos and other Contact Derm.treatment: Identify and remove allergen, topical steroids, oral prednisone for severe cases
Poison Ivy Photos and other Contact Derm.notes: Reaction from contact with irritant or allergen (e.g., poison ivy, nickel).

Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.treatment: Minoxidil, finasteride (for AGA), corticosteroids (for alopecia areata)
Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.notes: May be autoimmune (alopecia areata) or androgenetic. Can be emotionally distressing.

Tinea Ringworm Candidiasis and other Fun.treatment: Topical antifungals (clotrimazole, miconazole), oral fluconazole for resistant cases
Tinea Ringworm Candidiasis and other Fun.notes: Fungal infections are contagious. Candidiasis may indicate immune compromise.

Exanthems and Drug Eruptions.treatment: Discontinue offending drug, antihistamines, steroids for severe reactions
Exanthems and Drug Eruptions.notes: Drug eruptions can range from mild to severe (e.g., SJS/TEN — see Critical).

Warts Molluscum and other Viral Infectio.notes: HPV-related warts and molluscum are contagious. Usually self-limiting in healthy patients.

Light Diseases and Disorders of Pigmenta.treatment: Topical steroids, tacrolimus, phototherapy, depigmentation agents
Light Diseases and Disorders of Pigmenta.notes: Often autoimmune. Significant psychosocial impact possible.

Cellulitis Impetigo and other Bacterial.treatment: Oral antibiotics (penicillins, cephalosporins); IV antibiotics for severe cellulitis
Cellulitis Impetigo and other Bacterial.notes: Cellulitis can spread rapidly and lead to sepsis if untreated. Prompt treatment essential.

Bullous Disease Photos.notes: Autoimmune blistering. Can be extensive and life-threatening. Hospitalization may be needed.

Herpes HPV and other STDs Photos.treatment: Antivirals (acyclovir, valacyclovir for HSV/VZV); HPV vaccine for prevention
Herpes HPV and other STDs Photos.notes: Herpes is lifelong (latent). HPV can cause cervical cancer. Partner notification needed.

Scabies Lyme Disease and other Infestati.treatment: Permethrin 5% for scabies; doxycycline for Lyme disease
Scabies Lyme Disease and other Infestati.notes: Lyme disease (tick-borne) requires urgent antibiotic treatment to prevent chronic disease.

Lupus and other Connective Tissue diseas.notes: Systemic lupus can affect kidneys, heart, lungs. Requires specialist management.

Systemic Disease.treatment: Treat underlying systemic condition; dermatology + internal medicine co-management
Systemic Disease.notes: Skin findings may indicate serious internal disease (diabetes, liver disease, etc.).

Vascular Tumors.treatment: Propranolol for infantile hemangioma; surgery/radiation for malignant types
Vascular Tumors.notes: Requires expert evaluation — benign (hemangioma) vs. malignant (angiosarcoma) distinction is critical.

Vasculitis Photos.treatment: Corticosteroids, immunosuppressants (cyclophosphamide, rituximab) based on type
Vasculitis Photos.notes: Inflammation of blood vessels. Can affect organs. Requires rheumatology involvement.

Actinic Keratosis Basal Cell Carcinoma a.treatment: Surgical excision, Mohs surgery, photodynamic therapy, topical 5-FU / imiquimod
Actinic Keratosis Basal Cell Carcinoma a.notes: ⚠️ MALIGNANT. Basal cell carcinoma is most common skin cancer. Actinic keratosis is pre-malignant. URGENT referral required.

Melanoma Skin Cancer Nevi and Moles.treatment: Wide local excision, sentinel lymph node biopsy, immunotherapy (pembrolizumab, nivolumab), targeted therapy (BRAF inhibitors)
Melanoma Skin Cancer Nevi and Moles.notes: 🚨 MOST DANGEROUS SKIN CANCER. Can metastasize rapidly. IMMEDIATE specialist referral. Use ABCDE criteria (Asymmetry, Border, Color, Diameter, Evolving).
